piiConfigPanel.modeTokenizeDesc: Replace PII with indexed tokens before sending; restore the original details in the response.
piiConfigPanel.modeMaskDesc: Replace PII with generic placeholders. One-way — the original details are not recoverable.
piiConfigPanel.modeBlockDesc: Reject the message outright when PII is detected. The user sees a block notice instead of a reply.
piiConfigPanel.patternsDescription: Pick which types of personal data the detector should look for.
piiConfigPanel.customPatternsDescription: Define additional patterns the built-ins do not cover. Each rule needs a name, a regular expression and the replacement text used in mask mode.
piiConfigPanel.customPatternInvalidRegex: This regular expression is not valid.
piiConfigPanel.previewDescription: Try the active configuration against real text. Detection runs on every keystroke.
piiConfigPanel.inputHint: Type or paste any sentence — PII is detected live.
piiConfigPanel.detectedHint: Hover each highlight to see what type of data the detector identified.
piiConfigPanel.tokenizedHint: PII replaced with indexed tokens. This is the only version the upstream model sees.
piiConfigPanel.maskedHint: PII replaced with generic placeholders. This is what gets sent and stored.
piiConfigPanel.aiHint: Editable — try moving tokens around to test the round-trip.
piiConfigPanel.restoredHint: Tokens swapped back to your original details. Green spans show what was restored.
piiConfigPanel.blockHint: PII detected — the message would be rejected and never reach the AI.
piiConfigPanel.blockBody: Your input contains data that the policy does not allow. Remove the highlighted items and try again.
piiConfigPanel.blockPassBody: The text would be sent through unchanged.
piiConfigPanel.noPii: No PII detected — the text would be sent through unchanged.
piiConfigPanel.noPatternsEnabled: No detection patterns are enabled. Turn at least one on above to see detections.
